name: Via Augusta - El Pla del Penedès Loop
description: Easy 9.1-mile mountain bike loop through the historic Via Augusta and Penedès wine region, featuring vineyards and cultural landmarks.
region_intro: Hiking around Subirats offers diverse landscapes, from the prominent Ordal Mountains to the varied terrain of Serra del Pi de Moió and Serra de les Planes. The region features a mix of natural beauty and historical sites, including ancient castles and hermitages. Trails traverse vineyards, pine forests, and open hillsides, providing panoramic views across the Penedès wine region. This area provides a range of routes suitable for various fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Subirats The most popular hiking route is Turó de les Mentides and Serra de les Planes Loop from Ordal, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers panoramic views and diverse terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sant Pere de Lavern and Sant Joan Salerm Loop, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.0 km) path. This trail visits historic hermitages and winds through the local countryside. Local hikers also love the PR-C 156: Ordal and Castell de Subirats Loop, an 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the Ordal Mountains and past Castell de Subirats, often completed in about 4 hours 3 minutes. Hiking around Subirats is defined by the Ordal Mountains, the Serra del Pi de Moió, and historical sites like Castell de Subirats. The network offers options for easy strolls, moderate loops, and more challenging treks. The routes in Subirats are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Subirats's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Subirats?
A: Subirats offers a diverse selection of over 25 hiking trails. These range from easy strolls through vineyards to more challenging treks in the Ordal Mountains, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families in Subirats?
A: Yes, Subirats has 10 easy hiking routes. These trails are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them ideal for a relaxed outing. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the easier options are often suitable for those with children.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Subirats?
A: Hiking in Subirats offers varied landscapes, including the prominent Ordal Mountains, the rolling terrain of Serra del Pi de Moió and Serra de les Planes, and extensive vineyards. You'll encounter pine forests, open hillsides, and enjoy panoramic views across the Penedès wine region.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Subirats?
A: Many of the trails in Subirats are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356817412/cueva-del-ordal-y-castillo-de-subirats-circular-por-la-sierra-del-pi-de-moio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Ordal y Castillo de Subirats — circular por la Sierra del Pi de Moió&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.5 km loop that explores natural and historical sites.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore on a hike in Subirats?
A: Subirats is rich in history. You can explore ancient castles like Castell de Subirats, often featured on routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356812839/pr-c-156-ordal-and-castell-de-subirats-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-C 156: Ordal and Castell de Subirats Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, there are historic hermitages, like those visited on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356829570/sant-pere-de-lavern-and-sant-joan-salerm-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pere de Lavern and Sant Joan Salerm Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any caves or unique natural features to discover on hikes?
A: Yes, the region features several interesting natural formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3915664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Riera Caves&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Muntanyeta&lt;/a&gt; are notable. The Cueva del Ordal is also a significant natural attraction that can be explored as part of hiking loops through the Ordal Mountains.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Subirats?
A: The hiking trails in Subirats are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, panoramic views, and the blend of natural beauty with historical intrigue found along the routes.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in Subirats for experienced hikers?
A: While most routes are moderate, there is at least one difficult trail available for those seeking a greater challenge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356549758/ascent-to-el-montcau-via-the-torre-de-l-ordal-circular-from-ordal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to El Montcau via the Torre de l'Ordal — circular from Ordal&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route with significant elevation gain, offering a more strenuous experience.

Q: What are some good viewpoints or scenic spots in Subirats?
A: Many trails in Subirats offer panoramic views, especially those traversing the Ordal Mountains or the Serra de les Planes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356553562/turo-de-les-mentides-and-serra-de-les-planes-loop-from-ordal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de les Mentides and Serra de les Planes Loop from Ordal&lt;/a&gt; is known for its expansive vistas. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1581449" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya del Moro&lt;/a&gt; is a popular viewpoint.

Q: Are there any mountain passes I can hike to in the Subirats area?
A: Yes, the region includes several mountain passes that can be part of your hiking adventure. Notable passes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1680213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu d'Aragall Pass (537 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4048004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt de les Bassioles&lt;/a&gt;, offering elevated perspectives and varied terrain.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Subirats?
A: Moderate hikes in Subirats vary in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1356553562/turo-de-les-mentides-and-serra-de-les-planes-loop-from-ordal" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de les Mentides and Serra de les Planes Loop from Ordal&lt;/a&gt;, which is 9.5 km, typically takes around 2 hours 53 minutes to complete.
tips: The passage of this small torrent is saved with a precarious metal plate, it is not very difficult, but after crossing it, the path makes a sharp turn to the right and as a precaution, I recommend dismounting from the bicycle and walking.

faqs.question: Is the Via Augusta - El Pla del Penedès Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this mountain bike loop is generally considered easy. While some sections might be unpaved or slightly uneven, they are manageable for riders with average fitness and basic mountain biking skills. The route is designed to be accessible for various cycling levels.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of paved roads, dirt tracks, and some less challenging trails. It's suitable for mountain bikes and even gravel bikes, offering a varied ride through the Penedès region.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Via Augusta - El Pla del Penedès Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail is designed with flexibility in mind. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, the individual stages of the broader Via Augusta route often start and end at train stations, which typically have parking facilities nearby. You can plan your start point accordingly.

faqs.question: Can I reach the trailhead by public transport?
faqs.answer: Yes, the broader Via Augusta route, of which this loop is a part, is designed so that its individual stages start and end at train stations. This offers excellent flexibility for riders to access different sections and make travel arrangements using public transport.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect stunning scenery characterized by the region's prominent vineyards, often described as a 'wine-growing mosaic' that changes colors with the seasons. The landscape is dotted with small rural centers, castles, watchtowers, farmhouses, and wineries, offering a picturesque blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

faqs.question: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The route follows a recreated path of the ancient Roman Via Augusta, one of the longest communication routes built during Romanization. You'll discover landscapes imbued with history, passing by historical and cultural points of interest, some dating back to Roman times. One specific highlight is the Crossing of the Rovira stream.

faqs.question: Is the Via Augusta - El Pla del Penedès Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally suitable for dogs, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through vineyards, agricultural areas, or near roads. Be mindful of local regulations and ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The Penedès region's landscape, with its 'wine-growing mosaic,' changes colors with the seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, with vibrant colors in the vineyards. Summer can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are advisable if riding during that season.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to mountain bike the Via Augusta - El Pla del Penedès Loop. It utilizes public roads, ancient drover's roads, and agricultural paths.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Via Augusta Penedès, Vilafranca - El Pla del Penedès, and the Penedès 360 Slow Bike routes, among others.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: As the name suggests, the 'Via Augusta - El Pla del Penedès Loop' is a circular route, meaning you will finish at your starting point without needing to retrace your steps.
